description: Stage 3 mechanism-level screen: one causal question per slot. SmearGate and BigramHash are separate candidates. H1 (NorMuon+WD) is bundled as a community-paired optimizer discipline package, not a mechanism-level test. No slot claims to test muP or FA3.

slots.name: baseline_control_a
slots.why: Primary control: MLP3x + seq2048 + grad_clip=0.3 baseline. No code patches.
slots.validates: The local patched code path can reproduce baseline quality.
slots.falsifies: All derivative screens are suspect because the control itself is unstable.
notes: Exact control for all seven candidates. Uses only env-var config.

slots.name: baseline_control_b
slots.why: Noise estimate for the baseline control.
slots.validates: Short-run control variance is small enough for aggressive pruning.
slots.falsifies: The screen horizon is too noisy to interpret small deltas.
notes: R0A vs R0B spread calibrates kill thresholds.

slots.name: normuon_wd
slots.why: Community-paired optimizer discipline package: NorMuon (per-row second-moment normalization) + MuonWD=0.04 (decoupled weight decay). Every submission beating us uses at least one. Bundled because the community always pairs them and the causal story is unified: tighter optimizer discipline -> smaller weights -> better quant.
slots.validates: Better val_bpb or better quant gap at matched wallclock.
slots.falsifies: NorMuon+WD together are not worth the complexity; the baseline Muon is already good enough.
notes: WD=0.04 is the community sweet spot (#180). NorMuon from #89, #173.
This is a package test, not a mechanism-level test. If it wins, follow-on should isolate NorMuon vs WD.

slots.name: smeargate
slots.why: Learned sigmoid gate blending each token embedding with its predecessor. Injects a cheap recurrence-like bigram prior. ~512 params. Used by 7 of top 10 submissions.
slots.validates: Better val_bpb at matched wallclock from the token-blending prior alone.
slots.falsifies: SmearGate without BigramHash is not worth the code change.
notes: Gate inits to sigmoid(0)=0.5, so starts at 50/50 blend and learns from there.
Separated from BigramHash to get clean attribution.

slots.name: bigram_hash
slots.why: 4096-bucket hashed token-pair lookup table (dim=128, projected to model_dim). Adds ~524K params of explicit bigram memory the model cannot learn in 10 minutes.
slots.validates: Better val_bpb at matched wallclock from explicit bigram features alone.
slots.falsifies: The extra ~524K parameters hurt quantization more than the bigram features help.
notes: Weights init to zeros, so starts as a no-op and learns during training.
Watch artifact size: ~524K extra params should still fit in 16MB with int8+zlib.
Separated from SmearGate to get clean attribution.

slots.name: qat_int6
slots.why: STE fake int6 quantization in the forward pass teaches the model to survive 6-bit compression. Straight-through estimator passes gradients unchanged. Reduces quant gap from ~0.015 to ~0.002.
slots.validates: Quant gap shrinks significantly; post-quant BPB improves even if pre-quant is slightly worse.
slots.falsifies: QAT hurts pre-quant quality too much to recover at export.
notes: QAT makes pre-quant val_bpb WORSE (model trains with noise). The win is in post-quant.
Kill rule: if pre-quant val_bpb worse by >0.01, the gap is too large to recover.
Promote rule: if pre-quant within 0.005 of control (gap recovers at export).

slots.name: orthoinit
slots.why: Orthogonal init on Q,K,V,MLP.fc matrices (gain=1.0) provides better initial geometry than Kaiming uniform. Used by top submissions #135, #162, #164.
slots.validates: Faster early convergence (steps 50-100) and better final val_bpb.
slots.falsifies: Early convergence advantage disappears by step 300; Kaiming is good enough.
notes: Only affects matrices with min(shape) >= 64. Zero-init projections are unchanged.
This tests orthogonal init only, NOT muP scaling (muP is not implemented).
Watch: early learning curve shape, not just final value.

slots.name: label_smoothing
slots.why: Label smoothing=0.05 softens targets, regularizes logits toward smaller magnitudes, which should improve quantization friendliness. No community PR tests this, so it covers an unexplored region.
slots.validates: Better post-quant val_bpb due to smaller logit magnitudes.
slots.falsifies: Label smoothing hurts the model's ability to assign confident predictions.
notes: Wildcard hypothesis. No community PR tests label smoothing.
Watch: pre-quant vs post-quant gap. Smoothing should reduce the gap.

slots.name: compile_autotune
slots.why: torch.compile mode=max-autotune may find faster CUDA kernels, increasing steps reached in fixed wallclock.
slots.validates: ms/step improves by >5% or total steps increases meaningfully.
slots.falsifies: max-autotune has higher compile overhead that eats the kernel gains.
notes: Pure throughput test. No warmup change (that is a separate env var, testable independently).
Kill: if ms/step is SLOWER (compile regression).
Promote: if ms/step improves by >5%.
